JV Email Sequence
Okay. Now, I'm going to walk you through the JV sequence that I use for PLF.
The sequence was done completely via email and these are the emails that I sent out to
my partners. There are 39 emails, that's a lot of email and I go overboard. We do really,
really well with our JV promotions and we go absolutely overboard. What you're seeing
is about as intense as it gets.
No one else really does it like we do. We get great results our partners are used
to getting a lot of emails. They use to go on through a lot of launches. If you're in a
market where you don't have a lot of JVs that have been really trained to do really well
in a lot of launches. They might get overwhelmed by these number of emails, but ... I'm
just showing you one end of the spectrum and most intense end of the spectrum. But
you're going to see how ...
a lot of the triggers that we use in our pre-launch to our prospects, I use in my
sequence to my partners. Again, this sequence is done purely via email. We didn't use
any videos or anything. Of course, there's a lot of phone calls, individual emails and text
messages going out to our top partners, but this is what we sent out to all of our
partners and this is what we really used to drive the competition among our partners.
This is the first one and I will give you a copy of all these emails. If it's too small
for you to read, I’m doing my best to get them to fit on this screen. If they're too small
to read, you can read all of them. Also, I'm not going to go line by line through all 39 of
these emails. We're going to skip through to them pretty quickly.
I started off in this first email went out on June 3rd. This is June 3rd, a full three
months before our launch was to start and that's because in my market these ... The
launches tend to get scheduled out pretty far in advance and all the partners have very
busy calendars, promotional calendars. So I wanted to sort of mark those dates as far
ahead as I could. We sent this out three whole months now on June 3r and you can see
it's just a save the date message.
You can see even in that second paragraph, I start off with the authority. After
nine consecutive, seven figure launches, this is going to be the best launch yet. Just
wait until you see what I've got in my sleeve. I'm building a little bit of anticipation and
curiosity but have also massive authority with those nine consecutive launches.
Then, if you look down like in that PS section, if you promoted for me in the
past, you know my stuff converts like crazy every single time and I would love to have
you on board. Then, further down the last PPS, if you know of anyone else who's
thinking of launching real estates, do them a favor and warn them to move their dates.
Pretty much everyone in the known universe will be on board and supporting us on
those dates. Just saying.
Obviously, I’m trying to make sure I get those dates to myself and I don't overlap
with any other people in the market. That's just pure authority. This email is a save the

-- 2 of 10 --

date and it's a pure authority email. Then, about a month later and July 1st, this is
basically the exact same email for the most part. It's just another save the date, trying
to stay in front of my partners trying to get ... Just so that they don't ... So that they've
carved out those dates.
Also, I’m trying to make sure that no one else does a launch at that same time,
because if other people are doing a launch at that time, then they could be potentially
diluting the partners for me. If my partners are supporting someone else, they won't be
supporting me. I'm just trying to mark off my space on the calendar.
Then, on August 4th, about a month later, we did the June 3rd, July 1st, August
4th, we came out with ... Again, it's a very similar, well, somewhat similar email, it's a
save the date, but this is the first time I tell them the all new products PLF 4.0. It's the
big reveal. First, all new Product Launch Formula since 2010. Biggest JV prices ever.
Nine consecutive million dollar launches and just some more authority, but then also a
little bit of reason why here. That is all new and it's going to be a big launch.
Then, at August 16th, so this is about two weeks later. Now, we're only about
a ... August, we're only about a month out from the started of pre-launch and so now, I
go from every month to every second week. This is just another quick reminder to save
the date.
Then on August 22nd, now, we're getting close. I'm telling them, "Holy cow,
we're getting close, we're getting close. We're starting in September 4th." Then now on
August ... I'm sorry, September 1st and this is just a few days out now. This is only
three days out so the urgency and the frequency increases. Now, look at that subject
line, it's $220,000 this Wednesday. I just tell them we're going to have the biggest
launch price we've ever offered before and it's going to absolutely crush. This will be
our 10th consecutive million dollars plus launch.
Now, we're basically emailing them pretty much every day I think at this point.
Now, it’s September 2nd. Twelve reasons this launch will crush. Now, I'm starting to ...
We're getting a little more creative. It's not the same old email. I gave them 12 reasons
why it's going to completely crush and this starts off with this important header. Pre-
launch starts Wednesday. That's just two days. We start in Wednesday, September 4th.
The thing is your affiliate partners and JV partners are very, very busy. That's
why we try to very, very explicit. It starts Wednesday. That's in just two days.
Wednesday, September 4th. Then, now I know you get a lots of hype-filled JV, emails
almost every day. They tell you about promotions or launches are just going to kill it.
The problem is that a lot of them don't actually kill it, some of them just plain suck.
That's the truth.
Everyone hypes it up about how great their launch is going to be, but ours are
really great every single time. They just are. I'm just saying. They really are. So let's
forget the hype. Here's 12 reasons it's on to a crush. PLF is proven. I have personally

-- 3 of 10 --

done ... I've done nine consecutive million or multi-million dollar launches for the PLF
brand. Market loves it and I know how to sell it.
Number two, I’ve personally done 14 consecutive million or multi-million dollar
launches. This is in my first rodeo. That includes the ones I've done for other people for
clients. To my knowledge, PLF is the highest selling internet marketing product of all
time. I've sold over 20 million dollars worth. PLF 4 is an all new version. PLF has been
off to the market for a full year. The market is hungry for it and I just go on reason,
after reason, after reason all the way through. Just sort of hitting all the possible
objections why someone might not promote.
Then on September 3rd, it starts tomorrow, $4000 leads, $100,000 sales and this
is where I tell them about a $4000 price in leads for the most leads for just one day.
This is what we call sprint contest. It's a JV sprint contest. A sprint contest is something
I named it after like ...
They do this in bicycle racing like in the Tour De France. there's many races
within a race in the tour. There's a race every single day and then there's an overall race
for all 21 or 22 stages. But then, even within each day, they'll be little intermediate
sprints where it'll be like the top of this mountain or they'll be a sprint somewhere in the
middle. Two or three sprints in the middle a day and those sprints you'll get points for
and you'll also get money for.
I created that concept of sprint prices within a launch for joint venture partner.
Here, just in the first day, whoever drove the most leads, gets ... There's $4000 in
prizes, $2000 for first place, $1000 for second, $500 for third, and $250 each for 4th
and 5th. And then I tell them there's also $100,000 first prize, prizes go ...
This email, the day before we start out the pre-launch is all about the prizes.
Then, I send them email, this is on September 4th, the day the pre-launch goes live
and I sent this to them early in the morning before we actually went live. I gave them a
synopsis what this first video is all about, so they could start working on their copy.
Then, we actually give them swipe copy down below, so this is a copy they can cut and
paste.
The reality is most partners, especially with top partners, they are not going to
cut and paste and send this out. But, you want to give them this copy so they have
something to work with. So they have some place to start their copy. Again, on
September 4th, once the video actually went live, we send them an email to tell them it
was live. Give them their link in a swipe copy.
Then Later that day, that afternoon, we tell them they're crushing it. We got a
60.2 percent opt-in rate and we gave them basically an intermediate leader board for
the sprint contest. You can see we give them a current leader board where we listed
the top seven.

Then, this was sent on September 5th. This is basically talking about what a

This is going to be a sprint contest over a 48 hour period. Then here, this is just

Then, here we go to ... Basically this goes to September 8th there and this one

I didn't want to take the time and tell them about who won. You got to think that
your partners are very busy. I always think like there's only about one thing I can really
truly accomplish in each email and this email, I wanted to get them all amped up about

Moving forward, September 10th, this is about all the great leads we got

Now, I give the overall leader board.
This isn't like a rigid formula that you have to follow. I'm just sort of giving you
some of the psychology here. There's only so much you can fit in in each update but I
want to be updating them every single day. So in this day, like, "Here, this was on the
9th when we released that video. Okay. We're killing it, and the 9th, we're killing it.
Now, the 10th, okay, what do I have to talk about?" "We've got a lot of leads
